data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260514T190333-2cb05d63/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260514T190333-2cb05d63/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260514T190333-2cb05d63/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260514T190333-2cb05d63/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260514T190333-2cb05d63/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="fizeau-8134d6db">
    <title>routing: honor caller exclusions and fast-fail on transport timeouts</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
Fizeau wastes 2m45s per call retrying a dead local endpoint (4 attempts with 1s/2s/4s/8s backoff + per-attempt 30s dial timeout), then surfaces a provider_connectivity error to the caller. Even when the caller (DDx) records the failed (provider, model) and asks for re-routing on the next attempt, Fizeau re-routes to the same dead endpoint because the caller has no way to express "exclude this (provider, model)" in the request, and routing decisions are made in isolation from caller-side health signals. Observed reproduction: with bragi:1234/qwen3.5-27b unreachable, a DDx drain of 12 beads attempted each bead, every bead routed to bragi/qwen3.5-27b, every bead spent ~2m45s in Fizeau retries, every bead failed identically. Net drain time ~33 minutes, zero beads completed.

ROOT CAUSE
Two contributing behaviors in internal/provider/* and internal/serviceimpl/*:
1. Connect-time errors (dial tcp ECONNREFUSED / ETIMEDOUT to a local IP) are treated the same as in-flight provider errors and run through the full retry curve (4 attempts × ~30s = 2m45s). A local-network dial that misses the SYN window will not succeed on subsequent attempts; the retry burns wall-clock for no value.
2. The route decision in internal/routehealth and the public RouteRequest type carry no caller-supplied exclusion list. Routes recorded as failed by the caller cannot be expressed to Fizeau. The internal routehealth Store tracks process-local feedback only — fine for within-call retries, but stateless across calls.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add ExcludedRoutes []ExcludedRoute to the public RouteRequest, where ExcludedRoute names (Provider, Model, optional Endpoint). The router must skip any candidate matching the list. Document in service.go and the routing API doc.
- For connect-time errors (errors.As to *net.OpError with Op=="dial" AND ECONNREFUSED/ETIMEDOUT/EHOSTUNREACH), bypass the retry curve: 1 attempt, fail fast within ~5s. Reserve the retry curve for in-flight 5xx / partial-response errors that may transient retry usefully.
- When the request lists multiple viable endpoints for the same model (current or future endpoint-first config), on connect-failure to one endpoint, immediately try the next endpoint in the same call rather than surfacing failure. The order matches the existing route-decision strategy.
- Expose a routing-quality event recording "route excluded by caller hint" so observability can distinguish caller-driven re-routes from internal health signals.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not redesign provider config syntax. ExcludedRoutes is additive; endpoint-list support can land separately.
- Do not change the existing routehealth in-process Store contract; only extend RouteRequest.
- Do not alter retry behavior for non-transport-class provider errors (5xx, rate limit, content-policy) in this bead.

DEPS
None at Fizeau; DDx-side bead to actually populate ExcludedRoutes from execute-loop-failed-routes is filed separately under ddx (ddx-8a7a6843 covers the DDx escalation-signaling side).
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. TestRouteRequest_ExcludedRoutesSkipsCandidate asserts a candidate matching an entry in RouteRequest.ExcludedRoutes is filtered out of the route decision.
2. TestRouteRequest_ExcludedRoutesAllowsOtherCandidates asserts non-matching candidates remain selectable.
3. TestProviderDialError_FailsFastBelow5Seconds asserts that a dial-tcp-refused / dial-tcp-timeout error returns from the provider call in under 5 seconds (no 4-attempt retry curve).
4. TestProviderInflightError_StillRetries asserts in-flight 5xx errors keep the existing retry behavior (regression guard).
5. go test ./... -count=1 passes.
6. CHANGELOG.md and docs reference the new RouteRequest.ExcludedRoutes field and the connect-time fast-fail policy.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>area:routing, area:routehealth, kind:bug, spec:endpoint-first-routing</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="968997a560c7380ded2e8c36a02a09ad97cb2c23" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260514T190333-2cb05d63"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
All done. Here's a summary of what was delivered for bead `fizeau-8134d6db`:

**AC-1 & AC-2 — `RouteRequest.ExcludedRoutes`:**
- Added `ExcludedRoute` struct (`Provider`, `Model`, `Endpoint`) to `service.go`
- Added `ExcludedRoutes []ExcludedRoute` to `RouteRequest`
- Added matching `ExcludedRoute` type and `ExcludedRoutes` field to `internal/routing.Request`
- Added `FilterReasonCallerExcluded = "caller_excluded"` constant
- Filtering logic in `buildHarnessCandidates` skips matching candidates and marks them with the new reason
- `service_routing.go` propagates from the public surface to the engine via `publicToRoutingExcludedRoutes`

**AC-3 — Connect-time fast-fail (`TestProviderDialError_FailsFastBelow5Seconds`):**
- `IsDialError` in `provider_error.go` detects `*net.OpError{Op:"dial"}` via `errors.As`
- New check in `loop.go` before `IsTransientError` fails immediately on dial errors (bypasses 4-attempt curve)

**AC-4 — In-flight 5xx regression guard (`TestProviderInflightError_StillRetries`):**
- Confirmed `503 Service Unavailable` still goes through the retry curve; test verifies provider is called twice (1 failure + 1 success)

**AC-5 — `go test ./... -count=1`:** 69 packages, 0 failures.

**AC-6 — CHANGELOG.md and docs:** `CHANGELOG.md` documents both `RouteRequest.ExcludedRoutes` and the connect-time fast-fail policy under `[Unreleased]`.
